name: Barley (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Barley (Hordeum vulgare) [Cereals and cereal products] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU, DUKE, MANUAL, PHENOL EXPLORER, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170283 (Barley, hulled, SR Legacy) — macronutrients, sugars, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins
MacLeod 1954 J Inst Brew doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1954.tb02747.x; MacLeod 1953 doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1953.tb06210.x — free sugars & fructosans
LaBerge 1973 J Inst Brew doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1973.tb03568.x — free-sugar changes during maturation
Henry 1988 J Inst Brew doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1988.tb04560.x — barley carbohydrate review
Kim/Peukert et al. GC-MS metabolomic profiling of wheat/barley/oat/rye PMC5302243 — organic acids (malate, alpha-ketoglutarate highest in barley)
MDPI Crops 2024 4(4):38 doi:10.3390/crops4040038 — pre/post-milling barley organic & amino acid profiling
Bhatt 2021 (Indian barley phytic acid) ResearchGate 357614244; Dai 2007 doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2007.03.063 — phytic acid
Frontiers Sustain Food Syst 2023 doi:10.3389/fsufs.2023.1326716; PMC6963557 — beta-glucan
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~55 g/100g), with beta-glucan (~4.5 g) and total fiber (17.3 g) as major fermentable polysaccharides; free sugars are minor and led by sucrose (~0.6 g). The organic-acid class (malic dominant, then citric/succinic/fumaric/alpha-ketoglutarate) was entirely missing from the FooDB profile and is the key addition, though authoritative absolute mg/100g values are not published for raw grain (added with null amounts + rank notes). Nitrogen is chiefly protein-bo
curation_added: malic acid (dominant organic acid) + citric, succinic, fumaric, alpha-ketoglutaric acids (organic-acid class entirely absent before)
starch (~55 g/100g) and total dietary fiber (17.3 g) as the true dominant carbon sources
specific free sugars with amounts: sucrose (principal), glucose, fructose, raffinose, fructans
L-valine and L-serine (missing amino acids) + quantitative amounts for the full protein-derived amino-acid profile
quantitative minerals (Ca, Fe, Mg, P, K, Na, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se) and vitamins (B1,B2,B3,B5,B6,B9,E,K, choline)

curation_removed.name: Arsenate
curation_removed.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Co2+
curation_removed.reason: free cobalt ion; ultratrace FooDB artifact, barley grain has negligible cobalt/B12

curation_removed.name: Creatine cytosol
curation_removed.reason: animal metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant grain; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
curation_removed.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in a plant grain; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; plants supply carotenoids, not retinol; FooDB artifact

curation_removed.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
curation_removed.reason: oxidation artifact of methionine, not a genuine nutrient pool

curation_removed.name: Genistein
curation_removed.reason: soy isoflavone; not characteristic of barley; FooDB predicted/implausible

curation_removed.name: Isoflavones
curation_removed.reason: legume-type isoflavones not characteristic of barley; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
curation_removed.reason: furanocoumarin typical of Apiaceae/Rutaceae; implausible in barley; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: epsilon-Polylysine
curation_removed.reason: microbial fermentation product / food additive, not a barley grain constituent

curation_removed.name: Dimethylamine
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Methylamine
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Diethylamine
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Ethanamine
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Piperidine
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

components.name: starch
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170283 (by difference: carbohydrate 73.5 - fiber 17.3 - sugars 0.8); Henry 1988 doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1988.tb04560.x

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence_source: Kim/Peukert GC-MS PMC5302243; MDPI Crops 2024 4(4):38 doi:10.3390/crops4040038

components.name: ferulic acid
components.evidence_source: Idehen 2017 review; PMC7346127; colored-barley study doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2018.05.100 (45-1027 µg/g)

components.name: N-Methyltyramine, Dopamine, Serotonin, Histamine, Trigonelline (genuine barley biogenic amines/alkaloids at trace)

oxygen_note: food substrate; O2 availability is a simulation condition, not a property of the food
basis: per 100 g whole grain (hulled/dehulled barley kernel, ~10% moisture as-is)
